mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in CPIAUCSL above 220 indicates rising inflation, prompting the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates, which compresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and CPIAUCSL signal a tightening monetary policy amid rising inflation, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 9.0% signals a tightening labor market, which boosts consumer spending and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances where UNRATE falls below 9.0.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 500 thousand units indicates strong demand for residential construction, boosting economic growth and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST exceeds 500.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 90 signals robust manufacturing activity, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 90.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 130,000 indicates a strong labor market, which drives consumer spending and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 130000.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) above 13,438.8 billion dollars indicates sustained economic growth, which supports equity valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns despite a quarterly increase in GDPC1.
